notes: Invariant noun with the imperfective marker ka; a word-final n nasalises the preceding vowel, liman to limã, per maurer2009.
Word-initial prenasalised bilabial onset mb realises as mb; sa is the locative copula, per maurer2009.
The digraph tx realises as the affricate tʃ; a polar question is marked by intonation with no particle, per maurer2009.
Negation is framed by the clause-final marker fa; the vowel nasalises before a coda nasal, per maurer2009.
Word-initial ng realises as the prenasalised velar ŋɡ; the plural human subject ine takes no agreement, per maurer2009.
Habitual ka with a first-person subject clitic; an intervocalic r keeps a full alveolar rhotic, per maurer2009.

sentence: Kaxi me gaani mo kaxi te.
ipa: kaʃi me ɡaãi mo kaʃi te
gloss_en: My house is as big as your house.
notes: Equative comparison with mo and no copula between subject and adjective; x realises as ʃ, per maurer2009.
Identificational clause with the linker ki and no overt copula, per maurer2009.
The voiced labial-velar stop gb realises as ɡ͡b, a Niger-Congo substrate retention, per hagemeijer2009.
The voiceless labial-velar stop kp realises as k͡p, a Niger-Congo substrate retention, per hagemeijer2009.

gloss_en: Shark cartilage is a big thing.
notes: The voiced labial-velar stop gb flanks nasalised vowels; sa links subject and predicate, per hagemeijer2009.
A bare noun after the plural word ine takes no number suffix; sa is the locative copula, per maurer2009.
The anterior marker tava places the event before the reference time, per maurer2009.
A nominal predicate is joined by the copula sa; a word-final n yields the nasal vowel ũ, per maurer2009.
Bare-verb imperative; the object noun carries no article, per maurer2009.
Bare-verb imperative with a locative adverb, per maurer2009.
The prenasalised ng onset and the affricate tx co-occur; sa is the copula in a polar question, per maurer2009.
A copular clause is negated by the clause-final marker fa, per maurer2009.
A nominal predicate with the copula sa; a word-final n yields the nasal vowel ũ, per maurer2009.
The plural human subject ine with imperfective ka on an invariant object noun, per maurer2009.
